metadata.title: The Cowork Task Packet: Five Fields, One Scope

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The status-report failure taught me the lesson cold: the prompt said 'summarize the weekly notes' but never which week, so Cowork guessed and pulled last quarter in. Help me convert my own recurring report into a real packet: (1) fill the five fields — inputs named, folder access limited, forbidden actions, output file, verification criteria; (2) write it as the first paragraph of the task prompt the way the weekly-notes example does; (3) give me the after-the-run spot-check list, including how many tracker items to verify against the notes.

props.command: The status-report failure taught me the lesson cold: the prompt said 'summarize the weekly notes' but never which week, so Cowork guessed and pulled last quarter in. Help me convert my own recurring report into a real packet: (1) fill the five fields — inputs named, folder access limited, forbidden actions, output file, verification criteria; (2) write it as the first paragraph of the task prompt the way the weekly-notes example does; (3) give me the after-the-run spot-check list, including how many tracker items to verify against the notes.
props.segment: The Cowork Task Packet: Five Fields,

beats.narration_text: He asked Cowork to assemble the weekly status report. He pasted the prompt from last week. Cowork produced a report — longer than usual, with items from last quarter in the summary. The source folder had accumulated three months of notes. The prompt said 'summarize the weekly notes.' It did not say which week. Without the packet, the task had no scope. Cowork guessed. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, summarize weekly notes without scope

props.command: summarize the weekly notes and produce a status report

beats.narration_text: The task packet has five fields. Inputs named: the specific files or folders Cowork may read. Folder access limited: the explicit paths — not broader than needed. Forbidden actions: no sending, no sharing, no accessing paths not listed. Output file specified: name and location. Verification criteria implicit or stated: what makes the output correct. These five fields are what separate a scoped task from a guess.
beats.new_visual_element: Five packet fields — inputs / access limits / forbidden actions / output spec / verify

beats.narration_text: In practice, the packet is the first paragraph of the task prompt. 'Read the twelve meeting notes in slash weekly-notes and the tracker dot csv in slash project-data. Extract: completed items this week, items in the tracker not mentioned in any note, and action items with owners not in the tracker. Format as three-section markdown. Save to slash project-data slash outputs. Do not access any other folders. Do not send or share anything.' That is a scoped task.
beats.new_visual_element: Packet example — explicit paths, three-section output, forbidden actions stated
beats.spark_line: Name it. Bound it. Save it.

beats.narration_text: The verification step comes after the run, but the criteria belong in the packet. You open the saved report. Does it have all three sections? Spot-check five tracker items against the notes. Check the action items list — are the owners correct? Are sensitive details exposed that should not appear? The verification criteria you write before the run are the checkable conditions you use after it. Without them, verification is just reading and hoping.
beats.new_visual_element: Verification gate — section count / spot-check / owner check / exposure check

beats.narration_text: A Cowork task without a written packet is guessing in both directions — Cowork guesses what it should read, and you guess whether the output is right. The packet collapses both guesses into a specification. What Cowork reads is named. What it may not read is named. What the output must contain is named. What verification looks like is named. The packet costs three minutes. The absent packet costs an afternoon of wrong output and a re-run.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — packet = named inputs / bounded access / stated criteria / checkable output

beats.narration_text: Write your next Cowork task as a packet. Paste this.

props.command: Help me write a Cowork task packet. My task is: [describe it]. Walk me through five fields: (1) inputs with exact file names or folder paths, (2) access limits — what Cowork may not read, (3) forbidden actions — what it may not send, share, or do, (4) output specification — name and location of the result file, (5) verification criteria — what I will check to confirm the output is correct.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. A Cowork task without a written packet is guessing in both directions — Cowork guesses what it should read, and you guess whether the output is right. The packet collapses both guesses into a specification. What Cowork reads is named. What it may not read is named. What the output must contain is named. What verification looks like is named. The packet costs three minutes. The absent packet costs an afternoon of wrong output and a re-run. Write your next Cowork task as a packet. Paste this.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [The Cowork Task Packet: Five Fields, One Scope] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
